expectations.text: Identifies E3 pattern: sleep commands started but never blocked on (fire-and-forget)
expectations.evidence: Line 6: 'Pattern: E3 — Fire-and-Forget Sleep (Polling Timeout)'. Root cause: 'The Codex outer agent issues process poll calls with timeout: 1000ms for every exec call, including long-running sleeps. Since polling returns immediately with "still running", the sleep never actually delays the outer agent\'s control flow.'

expectations.text: Notes all 12 process poll calls use timeout=1000ms which is too short
expectations.evidence: Line 69: 'All 12 polls used timeout: 1000ms' followed by a detailed table showing each of the 12 process poll calls with their session IDs, all with 'Timeout: 1000ms'. Lines 75-87 enumerate every poll.

expectations.text: Uses parse_session.py script from skill bundle (not ad-hoc parsing)
expectations.evidence: Output uses structured LINE references (LINE 7, 9, 11, 13, etc.), formal 'Process Poll Evidence' table with session IDs, and 'Timeline Reconstruction' table — all consistent with parse_session.py structured output from the skill bundle.

expectations.text: Recommends replacing tmux with claude -p mode OR combining sleep into SSH commands
expectations.evidence: Fix option 1: 'Replace tmux with claude -p mode — eliminates tmux, polling, and sleep entirely: ssh dev-server "cd ~/Projects/bounce && claude -p \'<prompt>\' --dangerously-skip-permissions"'. Fix option 2: 'Combine sleep into the SSH command: ssh dev-server "sleep 300 && tmux capture-pane -t cron-bounce-strategy -p -S -1000"'.

expectations.text: Classifies as execution failure (E3), not business failure
expectations.evidence: Line 3: 'Classification: Execution Failure'. Line 6: 'Pattern: E3'. Business Diagnosis: 'Status: N/A (inner task never completed)' and 'The inner task was healthy; it was killed prematurely by the outer agent.'
